reforms.description: The Hungarian government introduced a science and technology action programme in 2000, which included five national R&D initiatives aimed at enhancing domestic inventions and innovations in specific fields such as pharmaceutical research and biotechnology. Additionally, the government aims to enhance the public R&D system by increasing the joint financing of research projects between the private and public sectors, with a goal to double R&D expenditures in GDP from 0.8% to 1.6%, strengthening public research facilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiatives aim to enhance R&D capabilities and expenditures, which are expected to boost innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The science and technology action programme was introduced and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a comprehensive action programme and the goal to double R&D expenditures represent significant structural changes.
reforms.source_quote: The objective of the government is to make the public R&D system... more responsive to market needs, by increasing the private/public joint financing of research projects.

reforms.description: The Hungarian government implemented new incentives to increase the mobility of researchers between universities and the private sector, aiming to improve the human resources available for R&D. Additionally, innovative 'training-in-service' programmes have been introduced, providing grants directly to schools and teachers to purchase services from preferred training providers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing researcher mobility and training is expected to build a more skilled workforce, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The incentives and training programmes have been put into practice and are currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of mobility incentives and training programmes represents a significant but narrower reform in human capital.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced new incentives to increase mobility of researchers between universities and the private sector.

reforms.description: Hungary is upgrading its Research and Development infrastructure to meet emerging economic and societal needs, particularly in light of EU accession requirements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Upgrading R&D infrastructure is essential for enhancing research capabilities and meeting future demands.
reforms.status_evidence: The upgrade plans have been announced but are not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The infrastructure upgrade is significant but does not represent a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: The need for upgrading the Research and Development infrastructure of the country...

reforms.description: The Hungarian government approved an R&D action plan aimed at restructuring the science system with a focus on cross-disciplinary research and improving linkages between research centers and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Restructuring the science system to enhance collaboration between research and industry is expected to drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The action plan has been approved and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The action plan represents a significant effort to improve the science system but is not a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The government approved an R&D action plan, including support of research centres to improve linkages between industry and universities.

reforms.description: The Szechenyi Plan aims to promote small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) by providing them with access to supplier contracts, industrial parks, and financial incentives such as tax breaks and grants.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting SMEs through financial incentives and access to resources is expected to foster innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Szechenyi Plan has been launched and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Szechenyi Plan represents a major initiative to support the startup ecosystem and enhance SME growth.
reforms.source_quote: The one-year old economic development programme of the Hungarian government, known as the Szechenyi Plan, includes promotion of SMEs.
